name: BH (3BLD)
description: The BH (Beyer-Hardwick) method is an expert-level 3BLD (3x3 blindfolded) solving technique. It uses pre-memorized 3-cycle commutators for both edges and corners, allowing the solver to solve any piece cycle in a single optimized algorithm. This is the gold standard for competitive BLD solving and is used by virtually all top BLD solvers worldwide.

steps.name: Memorize Edges and Corners
steps.description: Memorize the complete cycle sequence for both edges and corners. Advanced memory techniques such as letter pairs, images, and memory palaces are used to encode the solving sequence rapidly.
tips: Letter pairs with vivid images dramatically improve memo speed
Memory palaces (method of loci) help maintain order
Practice memo speed as a separate skill from execution
Review and refine your letter pair list regularly

steps.name: Execute Edge 3-Cycles
steps.description: Solve all edges using pre-memorized 3-cycle commutators. Each pair of letters in the memo corresponds to one commutator that cycles three edge pieces. Every possible edge target combination has a specific optimized commutator.
tips: Learn commutators for all edge target pairs — there are hundreds
Start with intuitive commutators and optimize over time
Drill frequently used commutators for speed
Understand the structure of commutators (A B A' B') to derive forgotten ones

steps.name: Execute Corner 3-Cycles
steps.description: Solve all corners using pre-memorized 3-cycle commutators. Similar to edges, each memo pair triggers a specific corner commutator that cycles three corner pieces.
tips: Corner commutators are generally shorter than edge commutators
Learn to recognize corner twist cases that require special handling
Practice corner execution separately before integrating with edges
Parity handling (when edge and corner cycle counts differ) must be addressed

pros: Used by all top-level BLD solvers
Scales well to multi-blind and bigger cubes
cons: Takes months or years to fully learn all cases
Deriving forgotten commutators mid-solve is slow
